other_observers: I saw the bird with Andrew Spencer and Andy Boyce. Andrea Robinsong found it independently later in the day.; I do not recall who spotted the bird originally, but I believe I was the first to ID it correctly.
description: I here reproduce the relevant portions of my field notes verbatim: "About same size or slightly smaller than adjacent Pectoral Sandpipers, primarily white, gray and black but with orangely wash on sides of neck.sides of upper breast. Back, scaps and wing coverts maybe 80% basic--plain gray. Older feathers darker--flight feathers and a bar in the coverts. Blackish eye patch. Black stripe up back on neck joins dark crown starting to wear white in middle. Bill and legs all black. Bill thick (about as thick as the legs), straight-edged, slighly thicker at base than tip, blunt-ended. Underparts clean white except for distinct, rather fine gray streaks on flanks."Behaviors: Relevant field notes verbatim: "bird was with other shorebirds, walking in short emergent vegetation. Actively picking stuff off water."Call: Bird was silent.Plumage: juvenile molting into basic
